airports.title: Newark · EWR
airports.body: The closest major hub — about a **1-hour** drive to Whitehouse Station. Direct flights from most of the country and much of Europe.

airports.title: Philadelphia · PHL
airports.body: About **1 hr 15 min** away, and sometimes cheaper. JFK and LaGuardia are options too (≈ 1 hr 30 min) if the fare is right.

airports.title: By train from NYC
airports.body: NJ Transit’s Raritan Valley Line runs from New York Penn Station. Most of the day, ride to **Raritan** and grab a 10–15 minute car from there; on **weekday evenings (about 4–7 pm)** some trains run direct to the **Whitehouse Station** stop.

airports.title: Rent a car
airports.body: The easiest way to get around the countryside — pick one up at the airport. You won’t need it on the wedding night itself (see the shuttle).

airports.title: Shuttle to the venue
airports.body: We’ll run a shuttle between the hotel block and the venue on the evening of the wedding, so you can leave the driving to us. Times to come.

hotels.name: Hampton Inn, Clinton
hotels.notes: Our main room block — free breakfast, easy parking, and a straight shot to the venue. Booking link coming soon.

hotels.name: Lambertville & New Hope
hotels.notes: For a prettier base, the river towns of Lambertville and New Hope are full of inns, antique shops, and good coffee — a little farther out, but lovely.

todo.title: The town of Clinton
todo.body: A storybook Main Street on the river — the red mill, little shops, and good coffee, about fifteen minutes away.

todo.title: Round Valley
todo.body: A startlingly blue reservoir ringed by trails. Bring good shoes; the color of the water alone is worth the morning.

todo.title: Sunken Silo & Metropolitan
todo.body: A couple of local favorites a short drive off — a relaxed brewery and a well-loved spot for a good meal.

todo.title: Beneduce Vineyards
todo.body: A friendly local winery with a lovely tasting patio — an easy, pretty way to spend an afternoon.
